title: Eliminate authenticated React Query refetch loop and stabilize portfolio rendering

requirements.summary: Stop continuous UI flicker by removing the silent actor/identity-driven React Query invalidation/refetch loop while authenticated.
acceptanceCriteria: When logged in via Internet Identity, the dashboard no longer continuously flickers between default/fallback values and live values.
React Query Devtools/logs (or console logs already present in hooks) do not show repeated invalidation/refetch cycles happening continuously without user action.
Queries refetch only at their intended cadence (e.g., prices/FX at configured intervals) and not in a tight loop triggered by actor/identity changes.

file_operations.description: Remove the broad invalidateQueries + refetchQueries-on-every-actor-change effect that currently targets all non-actor queries. Replace it with a narrowly-scoped, identity-transition-aware strategy (e.g., invalidate only portfolio-related queries once when the authenticated principal changes, and do not force immediate refetch). Ensure actor/identity initialization does not continuously trigger global query refresh.

Prevent duplicate/competing access-control initialization side effects across hooks. Ensure access-control initialization (if still needed) happens in one place and does not trigger cascaded query invalidations/refetches (e.g., gate with a one-time-per-actor guard and avoid repeated initialization attempts on stable actor instances).

Align actor-dependent queries with actor readiness without causing refetch loops: tighten `enabled` conditions for authenticated data so queries do not run (or reset) while actor/auth is transitioning, and ensure query keys are stable and do not change due to non-primitive identity/actor objects.

requirements.summary: Keep portfolio UI stable during background price/FX refetches by retaining the last successful computed portfolio state instead of reverting to fallback/default mid-session.
acceptanceCriteria: During any background refetch of prices and/or exchange rate, the totals and asset rows do not visibly alternate between two different states (no oscillation).
If a live fetch fails, the UI continues to show the last known successful live/cached values (or a single consistent fallback), rather than rapidly switching back and forth.
The UI still updates to new live values when a subsequent fetch succeeds, without causing visible flicker.

file_operations.description: Adjust portfolio-related query behavior to retain last successful data during background refetches and transient failures: (1) for live prices and FX queries, avoid overwriting cached successful data with fallback/empty data on fetch errors (prefer keeping previous cache); (2) for derived portfolio computation, key recomputation off stable update markers (e.g., dataUpdatedAt timestamps) and keep the previous computed result while dependencies are refetching; (3) ensure initial fallback is used only for first render and not reintroduced once real data has been successfully computed.

requirements.summary: Add lightweight, non-spammy diagnostics that attribute any portfolio-related invalidation/refetch to authentication/actor readiness changes.
acceptanceCriteria: After page load, diagnostics indicate at most one portfolio-query invalidation/refetch caused by the actor becoming ready (not repeated continuously).
No new user-facing error banners are introduced as part of the diagnostics.

file_operations.description: Create a tiny diagnostics helper to emit clearly identifiable, de-duplicated console logs for React Query invalidation/refetch triggers (e.g., a function that logs a reason string once per identity/principal change and/or with simple time-based throttling).

Integrate the diagnostics helper to log a single, clearly labeled reason when any portfolio-related query invalidation is performed due to identity/actor becoming ready or changing. Ensure logs are de-duplicated and do not spam during normal operation.

Use the diagnostics helper to annotate any deliberate portfolio-related invalidation/refetch decisions that are tied to authentication/actor readiness (if any remain after REQ-1 changes), ensuring attribution is clear and not continuous.
